title: Transfer Learning Application of Self-supervised Learning in ARPES

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies spatially resolved micro-ARPES on Fe3Sn2 and asks how to automate labeling of many local band-structure cuts across a 4D dataset (x, y, angle, energy). It compares supervised transfer learning (fine-tuned ResNet50), unsupervised clustering (k-means on raw or learned features), and self-supervised representation learning (DINO, SwAV, MoCo, BYOL), concluding that self-supervised features are most useful when combined with a few-shot kNN labeling strategy, especially DINO with a single reference image per class.

figures.description: Conceptual evolution of ARPES from photoelectric effect to modern spatially resolved measurements.
1D energy-resolved photoelectron measurement (kinetic energy vs intensity).
Angle-resolved 2D detector measurement (energy and angle vs intensity).
3D ARPES acquisition by rotating the analyzer/sample to obtain two angular dimensions plus energy.
Spatially resolved 4D ARPES concept: real-space x,y scan combined with dispersion cut (angle, energy).
Pipeline for supervised learning: pretrained ResNet50 is fine-tuned on labeled Fe3Sn2 ARPES images.
Predicted label probabilities across the scanned Fe3Sn2 real-space map and a domain map from the maximum-probability label.
Clustering pipeline comparing direct flattening of ARPES images versus representation-space clustering with optional t-SNE reduction.
k-nearest-neighbor pipeline in representation space using k reference images per label and Euclidean distance.
Proposed experimental loop for semi-supervised labeling during acquisition: compare new images to references, alert expert on deviations, and add new labels if needed.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper states that micro-focused laser/synchrotron ARPES measurements were performed on Fe3Sn2, producing spatially resolved 4D datasets of (x, y, angle, energy vs intensity). The raw measured objects are ARPES band-dispersion images/cuts acquired at different real-space positions; the paper does not specify file formats, detector readout format, or exact instrument data structure.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed outputs include integrated-intensity real-space maps, labeled/predicted domain maps, fine-tuned ResNet50 probability maps, k-means cluster labels, and performance tables (accuracy, F1, recall, precision) for supervised, clustering, and kNN experiments. The paper also uses representation-space embeddings from pretrained self-supervised models and optional t-SNE projections as processed features for clustering.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The datasets generated during and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only as ARPES images / 4D ARPES measurements from Fe3Sn2 collected with micro-focused laser ARPES. No explicit raw file format, directory structure, or public repository is given.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include labeled ARPES images used for supervised fine-tuning, integrated-intensity maps, predicted probability maps, k-means cluster assignments, representation-space embeddings from pretrained models, and t-SNE-reduced coordinates. Quantitative results are summarized in Tables I-III.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data appear to exist at least for Fig. 2b (predicted probability/domain map) and the performance tables, but the paper does not state that source data files are deposited separately. No explicit source-data package is described.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: The paper mentions a primary code written for the computational experiment by one author, but does not state that scripts/notebooks are publicly provided. No repository link is given.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide a public dataset, explicit source-data files, or scripts. Data are only available on reasonable request, so replotting from the paper alone is not directly possible.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily image-based ARPES data with labels assigned by experts. The paper distinguishes raw ARPES images from processed representations and from final label maps/tables. It also uses pretrained computer-vision models (ImageNet-based) as transfer-learning feature extractors, meaning the analysis depends on both experimental data and external pretrained weights, but only the experimental data are discussed in the availability statement.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper explicitly says the study uses measured Fe3Sn2 ARPES 4D data and discusses raw ARPES images as inputs to supervised, clustering, and kNN pipelines.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figures and tables are derived from processed outputs such as predicted probability maps, cluster labels, and performance metrics; the paper also refers to analysis code and computational experiments.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper implies both raw measured ARPES images and processed figure/data products, but does not explicitly state public source-data deposition.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The datasets generated during and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.”
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to classify the schematic figures as measurement data. The paper is mostly about machine-learning workflows on ARPES images, not about a new physical phase or band-structure discovery. The only concrete dataset statement is request-based availability; no explicit raw/source-data repository is mentioned.
